channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Alvarez's 112th-minute goal helps lift Argentina past Switzerland 3-1 and into World Cup semifinals
item.description: Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.
item.content:encoded:
Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.

(Image credit: Abbie Parr)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Messi and Argentina survive another close call to reach the World Cup semifinals
item.description: Argentina was taken to the brink in its first two knockout games. In Saturday's quarterfinal against Switzerland, the Albiceleste survived again to advance to the semis, where they will face England.
item.content:encoded:
Argentina was taken to the brink in its first two knockout games. In Saturday's quarterfinal against Switzerland, the Albiceleste survived again to advance to the semis, where they will face England.

(Image credit: Odd Andersen)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: 200 young campers rescued as flooding hits parts of Missouri and Kentucky
item.description: A historic rainfall event has left communities across several Missouri counties underwater and prompted water rescues, including an evacuation of a summer camp.
item.content:encoded: A historic rainfall event has left communities across several Missouri counties underwater and prompted water rescues, including an evacuation of a summer camp.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: US attacks Iran over ship being hit in Strait of Hormuz; Tehran lashes out again at Gulf Arab states
item.description: The United States attacked Iran early Sunday morning over an Iranian attack on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran apparently responded with strikes targeting Bahrain, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates.
item.content:encoded:
The United States attacked Iran early Sunday morning over an Iranian attack on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran apparently responded with strikes targeting Bahrain, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In a nailbiter, England moves on to the World Cup semifinals, defeating Norway 2-1
item.description: England's Jude Bellingham has done it again. Scoring both of his team's goals in a thrilling quarterfinal against Norway that needed extra time. It was the first World Cup meeting between the two.
item.content:encoded:
England's Jude Bellingham has done it again. Scoring both of his team's goals in a thrilling quarterfinal against Norway that needed extra time. It was the first World Cup meeting between the two.

(Image credit: Richard Pelham)
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis

item.title: Palestinian aid worker who organized World Cup screenings killed in Israeli strike
item.description: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, a beloved aid worker in Gaza. He was killed by as Israeli airstrike while en route to a World Cup screening which he organized.
item.content:encoded: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, a beloved aid worker in Gaza. He was killed by as Israeli airstrike while en route to a World Cup screening which he organized.
item.dc:creator: Anas Baba

item.title: This English professor has run with the bulls in Spain for two decades
item.description: When Bill Hillmann was 19 years old, he read Ernest Hemingway's The Sun Also Rises. That book inspired him to pursue two dreams: a career in literature and to run with the bulls in Pamplona, Spain.
item.content:encoded: When Bill Hillmann was 19 years old, he read Ernest Hemingway's The Sun Also Rises. That book inspired him to pursue two dreams: a career in literature and to run with the bulls in Pamplona, Spain.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: Justice Department subpoenas New York Times reporters over Air Force One reporting
item.description: The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.
item.content:encoded:
The Times says federal agents turned up on the doorsteps of several of its journalists to force grand jury testimony next week over their coverage of the Air Force One plane gifted to Trump by Qatar.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: The biggest steam locomotive is whistle-stopping across the U.S.
item.description: Huge crowds of train fans turn out as the 1940s era Big Boy steam locomotive is making a rare trip cross country.
item.content:encoded:
Huge crowds of train fans turn out as the 1940s era Big Boy steam locomotive is making a rare trip cross country.

(Image credit: Michelle Gustafson for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Lunden

item.title: Opinion: Is democracy a joke? Count Binface says no.
item.description: Populist British MP Nigel Farage resigned from Parliament over questions about his finances, and is running for re-election in his constituency. His biggest rival? Count Binface.
item.content:encoded:
Populist British MP Nigel Farage resigned from Parliament over questions about his finances, and is running for re-election in his constituency. His biggest rival? Count Binface.

(Image credit: BENJAMIN CREMEL/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon
